channel.title: Science - Latest - Google News
channel.copyright: Copyright © 2026 Google. All rights reserved. This XML feed is made available solely for the purpose of rendering Google News results within a personal feed reader for personal, non-commercial use. Any other use of the feed is expressly prohibited. By accessing this feed or using these results in any manner whatsoever, you agree to be bound by the foregoing restrictions.

item.title: Earth-like LHS 1140b May Feature The First Atmosphere Found On Exoplanet - Hackaday
item.description:
1. Earth-like LHS 1140b May Feature The First Atmosphere Found On Exoplanet  Hackaday
  2. First atmosphere found on Earth-like planet in habitable zone of distant star  BBC
  3. Harvard scientists detect atmosphere on distant ‘Earth-like’ planet  Harvard University
  4. Astronomers discover 1st atmosphere around a rocky Earth-like planet in the habitable zone  Space
  5. Earth-like exoplanet found to have an atmosphere  The Guardian

item.title: Did Viking 1 find life on Mars 50 years ago? Here's why the debate continues - Space
item.description:
1. Did Viking 1 find life on Mars 50 years ago? Here's why the debate continues  Space
  2. Photos: 50 Years Since Viking 1 Landed on Mars  The Atlantic
  3. From Hampton to Mars: How NASA Langley Helped Land on the Red Planet  NASA (.gov)
  4. On This Day: Viking 1 is first spacecraft to land on Mars  upi.com
  5. Monday briefing: 50 years after Viking landed on Mars, did Nasa overlook evidence of life?  The Guardian

item.description:
1. Benin's presumed-dead coral reef rediscovered teeming with life  Oceanographic Magazine

  3. The Thriving Coral Reef That ‘Defeated Time’ in West Africa  Inside Climate News
  4. “It Was a Mix of Excitement and Disbelief”: When Scientists Sent Deep-Sea Rovers to Confirm a 1960s Theory, They Made a Surprising Discovery  The Debrief
  5. After More Than 60 Years Presumed-Dead A Coral Reef Is Alive and Thriving Off The Coast Of Benin  IFLScience

item.title: Earth’s Oceans Are Rapidly Losing Oxygen. It Could Destabilize the Planet - Gizmodo
item.description:
1. Earth’s Oceans Are Rapidly Losing Oxygen. It Could Destabilize the Planet  Gizmodo
  2. Earth’s waters are quietly running out of oxygen, scientists warn  ScienceDaily
  3. Oxygen "disappearing" from earth's waters at alarming rate, scientists warn  Oceanographic Magazine
  4. Underwater oxygen loss threatens earth's stability, researchers warn  Phys.org
  5. Oxygen is vanishing from Earth’s waters  Tech Explorist

item.title: James Webb Space Telescope discovers how black holes feed themselves - Space
item.description:
1. James Webb Space Telescope discovers how black holes feed themselves  Space
  2. NASA’s James Webb catches a supermassive black hole feeding  ScienceDaily
  3. NASA's James Webb Telescope Maps How Supermassive Black Holes Gain Mass  NDTV
  4. Webb Telescope Reveals How Supermassive Black Holes Keep Feeding Themselves Thanks to Gas Recycling  ZME Science
  5. The James Webb Space Telescope spent nearly eight hours staring into NGC 4696, a galaxy 145 million light-years away, and watched a river of cool gas pour into an 800-light-year disk spinning at 600 kilometers per second, feeding the supermassive black  Space Daily

item.title: Meteorite that crashed into NJ home holds building blocks of life, scientists say - Fox News
item.description:
1. Meteorite that crashed into NJ home holds building blocks of life, scientists say  Fox News

  4. Meteorite that crashed into New Jersey home contains "alien world chemistry," scientists say  CBS News
  5. ‘Alien world chemistry' discovered in New Jersey meteorite kept quiet for years  NBC New York

item.description:

  4. How and when to see the 2026 Perseid meteor shower  BBC
  5. Perseids return with weeks of meteor viewing before peak night  Beaumont Enterprise

item.title: The Universe Can Expand Faster Than Light Without Breaking Physics - SciTechDaily
item.description:
1. The Universe Can Expand Faster Than Light Without Breaking Physics  SciTechDaily
  2. The observable universe is roughly 93 billion light-years across despite being only 13.8 billion years old, because space kept expanding while the oldest light was travelling toward us  Space Daily

item.title: In 1977, NASA launched two probes designed to last five years. Voyager 1 and 2 are now the only human-made objects in interstellar space, still operating, still transmitting, funded by a team of engineers some of whom have worked on the mission for nearly 5 - Space Daily
item.description:
1. In 1977, NASA launched two probes designed to last five years. Voyager 1 and 2 are now the only human-made objects in interstellar space, still operating, still transmitting, funded by a team of engineers some of whom have worked on the mission for nearly 5  Space Daily
  2. Voyager 1 on track to reach one light-day from Earth  it-online.co.za
  3. NASA's Voyager 1 Radio Transmitter Is Weaker Than a Refrigerator Lightbulb. It Still Reaches Earth From 15 Billion Miles Away  19FortyFive
  4. Voyager 1 is still transmitting from more than 25 billion kilometres away, powered by less than 250 watts of decaying plutonium — a signal that now takes over 23 hours to reach Earth, carried by a radio no stronger than a refrigerator lightbulb  Make Tech Easier
  5. The engineers who built Voyager knew they would almost certainly be dead before it left the solar system, and they built it anyway, a gift from people who would never see where their work would go  ScienceBlog.com

item.title: The mysterious hum heard around the world may finally have an explanation - ScienceDaily
item.description:
1. The mysterious hum heard around the world may finally have an explanation  ScienceDaily
  2. Scientists finally solve mystery of buzzing sound 4% of world's population can hear  LADbible
  3. A mysterious hum has plagued the Earth for 50 years. Scientists may have finally found its source  MSN

item.title: Mysterious 'little red dots' at the beginning time may finally have an explanation, thanks to newfound 'little blue companions' - Live Science
item.description:
1. Mysterious 'little red dots' at the beginning time may finally have an explanation, thanks to newfound 'little blue companions'  Live Science
  2. One idea, two cosmic mysteries—linking Little Red Dots and globular clusters  Phys.org
  3. Little red dot galaxies may solve a cosmic neutrino mystery  The Brighter Side of News
  4. A mysterious “red dot” spotted in space: are we witnessing a cosmic birth?  Futura, le média qui explore le monde
  5. The James Webb Space Telescope found tiny “little red dots” that looked like impossibly compact galaxies — but astronomers now suspect many may be “black hole stars,” young black holes wrapped in gas so dense that the material falling into them produces  Space Daily

item.title: A radio structure thought for 40 years to tower over the Milky Way’s centre is actually a nearby stellar bubble — not the fossil scar of a black hole eruption - Space Daily
item.description:
1. A radio structure thought for 40 years to tower over the Milky Way’s centre is actually a nearby stellar bubble — not the fossil scar of a black hole eruption  Space Daily
  2. A Giant Structure Is Towering Over The Milky Way. After 40 Years, Scientists Have Worked Out What It Is  ScienceAlert
  3. Scientists solve 40-year mystery of the "lobe" above the center of the Milky Way  NEWS.am TECH

item.title: Astronauts returning from long stays in orbit say the smell of rain is almost too much to bear and a familiar street feels like something they are piloting rather than driving - Space Daily
item.description:
1. Astronauts returning from long stays in orbit say the smell of rain is almost too much to bear and a familiar street feels like something they are piloting rather than driving  Space Daily
  2. Why the smell of rain feels overwhelming to astronauts after spending months in space, according to Nasa  The Times of India
  3. Astronauts insist space has a smell — a mix of seared steak, hot metal and welding fumes that clings to a suit after every spacewalk  Space Daily

item.title: Physicists Have Argued Over This Problem Since 1883. It May Now Be Solved. - The New York Times
item.description:

  3. Feynman Sprinkler Problem Solved: Momentum Flux, Not Fluid Swirl, Spins Both Ways  Tech Times
  4. Mines researcher helps put “silly sprinklers” in reverse to unravel decades-old physics puzzle  Mines Newsroom
  5. Scientists used children’s ‘silly sprinklers’ to solve a physics problem Richard Feynman could not; the reverse version spins 50 times slower  The Times of India

item.title: In May 1973, Skylab reached orbit with its heat shield torn away, one solar wing gone and the other jammed, leaving America’s first space station at roughly 54°C — so engineers built a parasol in days and astronauts carried it up to save the laboratory - Space Daily
item.description: In May 1973, Skylab reached orbit with its heat shield torn away, one solar wing gone and the other jammed, leaving America’s first space station at roughly 54°C — so engineers built a parasol in days and astronauts carried it up to save the laboratory  Space Daily

item.title: We assume life needs a sun, but a team at Ludwig Maximilian University of Munich calculated in 2026 that moons orbiting starless rogue planets could sustain liquid oceans for up to 4.3 billion years — roughly as long as Earth has existed — without a single ra - Space Daily
item.description: We assume life needs a sun, but a team at Ludwig Maximilian University of Munich calculated in 2026 that moons orbiting starless rogue planets could sustain liquid oceans for up to 4.3 billion years — roughly as long as Earth has existed — without a single ra  Space Daily

item.title: One of the world's oldest orbicular granites is in India: The 2.56-billion-year-old Pichhore rock is baff - The Times of India
item.description:
1. One of the world's oldest orbicular granites is in India: The 2.56-billion-year-old Pichhore rock is baff  The Times of India
  2. Madhya Pradesh’s Pichore granite is 2.5 billion years old, shows study; among the oldest geological formations on Earth  Research Matters
  3. Geologists find 2.5 billion-year-old granite in Madhya Pradesh | Pichhore vulnerable to agricultural expansion | Inshorts  Inshorts
  4. Earth’s Second-Oldest Orbicular Rock Found in Madhya Pradesh  GK Today
  5. 2.5-Billion-Year-Old 'Time Capsule' Discovered: Earth's Second-Oldest Orbicular Rock Found In MP  Republic World
